content.theme: Toxicity is fuel. You inject it for power. You vent it through violence. The gap between injection and venting is the entire game.
content.design: The Leech is a self-buffing melee DPS who uses Toxicity as a volatile resource pool. Two branches — Furnace (Burn: inject Toxicity for escalating self-buffs) and Parasite (Bleed: vent Toxicity through melee hits for drain effects, debuffs, and contamination spread) — create a push-pull dynamic. The Leech's power ceiling scales with how close to collapse they're willing to operate.

content.label: Critical Rule
content.text: Burns inject Toxicity — they don't spend it. A paladin spends a pool downward. The Leech pushes a pool upward. Every Burn makes you stronger AND more dangerous to yourself. The power and the risk are the same action.

content.label: Critical Constraint
content.text: No accuracy from the Leech tree. Burns give damage dice, flat damage, speed, damage immunity — never accuracy bonuses. A Leech who misses is a reactor with no cooling system. You injected but couldn't vent. This forces cross-tree investment (Precision for setup, Dancer for more hit attempts, Shadow for accuracy triggers) or squad dependency (Influence buffs, Precision's Cracked). Accuracy isn't optimization for the Leech — it's survival.

content.label: vs Channeler
content.text: Channeler spends Toxicity + HP for ranged damage at a distance. Leech injects Toxicity into themselves for melee buffs — the Toxicity goes UP, not down. Channeler is glass cannon at range. Leech is volatile brawler in melee.

content.label: vs Sin Eater
content.text: Sin Eater uses Toxicity as a management puzzle — absorb from allies, redirect to enemies, cycle through the sweet spot. Leech uses Toxicity as rocket fuel — inject it, ride the high, hit things until the tank empties. Sin Eater is selfless support. Leech is selfish offense. They synergize perfectly: the Leech runs hot, the Sin Eater acts as their overflow valve.

content.label: vs Fury
content.text: Fury is an aggressive melee class fueled by rage (no explicit resource, just positioning and aggression). Leech is an aggressive melee class fueled by Toxicity (explicit resource with escalating penalties). Fury doesn't care about Toxicity. Leech IS Toxicity.
Core Mechanics
The Burn & Bleed Loop

At the start of your turn, you Burn — activate one or more Burn effects, voluntarily injecting Toxicity for self-buffs. Then you attack in melee. On hit, you Bleed — venting Toxicity into the target, reducing your Toxicity and dealing bonus damage. On a miss, nothing bleeds. You injected but couldn't vent.

Good round: Burn +3 Toxicity → hit twice → Bleed -2 each = net -1. Stable. Sustainable. Bad round: Burn +3 Toxicity → miss both = net +3. Climbing. Dangerous. Over a 5-round combat: You'll end 5-15 Toxicity higher than you started. That's by design. The Leech never gets clean during combat. The best you can do is manage the rate of climb.
Furnace Trigger (Burn Branch)

When you activate a Burn effect (voluntarily increase your Toxicity for a buff). Furnace augments enhance Burns — bigger buffs, cheaper costs, new Burn options, synergies between multiple active Burns. The Furnace branch makes you stronger but climbs your Toxicity faster.
Parasite Trigger (Bleed Branch)

When you Bleed Toxicity into an enemy on a melee hit. Parasite augments enhance Bleeds — more Toxicity vented per hit, bonus effects on Bleed (debuffs, HP drain, contamination spread, armor shred), and rewards for sustained melee contact. The Parasite branch makes your venting more efficient and adds offensive utility to every hit.
Combined

Burn injects. Hit vents. Both branches reward the same action loop: Burn → attack → Bleed. A Leech who invests in both has stronger Burn buffs AND more efficient Bleeds. The hybrid is the most powerful and the most dependent on landing hits — miss and you've got maximum injection with zero venting.
Toxicity Thresholds (Leech-Adjusted)
Standard Toxicity thresholds apply, but the Leech's augments and abilities shift them upward. A Leech with Toxic Resilience (+2 shift) effectively has:
The Leech's "operating range" is 5-12 where other classes are already feeling penalties. This is the design space — the Leech functions in a Toxicity zone that cripples everyone else.

content.label: Natural Counters
items: High Evasion enemies — misses are existential. No vent = climbing. Two misses in a row and you're in crisis.
Ranged kiting — Leech is melee-only for Bleed. Enemies that stay at range deny your venting entirely. You inject but can't vent. Timer is ticking.
Anti-Toxicity effects — anything that forces additional Toxicity gain (rift zones, enemy Toxicity auras) pushes you toward collapse faster. The environment attacks you.
Condition-heavy enemies — Stun prevents attacking. No attacks = no Bleeds = no venting. A stunned Leech at high Toxicity is a bomb that might go off.
Long fights — the Leech always climbs. Short fights (3-4 rounds) are manageable. Long fights (7+) push even conservative Leeches toward collapse. You need to end fights fast.
Isolation from Sin Eater — without the Sin Eater overflow valve, the Leech has no external Toxicity management. Solo Leech = you manage everything yourself.

content.text: AUGMENT TREE

content.title: Entry
content.budget: Both Furnace Core and Toxin Push are necessary. Burns with no Bleed = spike with no outlet. Bleed with no Burn = venting but gaining nothing. Most Leeches take both at T1. Toxic Resilience extends your operating range.

augments.name: Furnace Core
augments.effect: Unlocks Burn. 0 AP (free action, start of turn): inject +2 Toxicity → gain +1 melee damage die until your next turn. The foundation. Without this, you're not a Leech — you're just a melee fighter standing in a rift zone.

augments.name: Toxin Push
augments.effect: Unlocks Bleed. On melee hit: vent 1 Toxicity into the target. Deals 1 bonus damage (bypasses armor). The exhaust pipe. Without this, Burns have no outlet and you spike to collapse.

augments.name: Toxic Resilience
augments.effect: Toxicity penalty thresholds shift up by 2 (penalties start at 8 instead of 6, etc.). Your body tolerates what kills others. More operating room.

augments.name: Rift Appetite
augments.effect: Toxicity you gain from ambient/environmental sources (rift zones, enemy auras) heals 1 HP. Your body feeds on contamination. Others walk through a rift zone and get sicker. You walk through and heal.

content.title: Adapted
content.budget: Overdrive is the second Burn — running both Furnace Core + Overdrive injects +5 Toxicity/round for +1 damage die, +2 flat damage, +5ft speed. Powerful. Unsustainable without consistent hits.

augments.name: Overdrive
augments.effect: New Burn (stacks with Furnace Core). 0 AP: inject +3 Toxicity → gain +5ft movement speed AND +2 flat melee damage until next turn. Running both Burns = +5 Toxicity/round for +1 damage die, +2 flat damage, +5ft speed. Powerful. Unsustainable without consistent hits.

augments.name: Volatile Injection
augments.effect: When you Burn, your next melee attack this turn ignores 2 armor. The injection destabilizes your rift energy — the next thing you hit eats raw contamination through their plating.

augments.name: Siphon Strike
augments.effect: Bleed upgrade: each melee hit vents 1 Toxicity as 2 bonus damage (bypasses armor) instead of 1 damage. Additionally: per 3 Toxicity accumulated on a single target, that target gets -1 accuracy (cumulative, lasts rest of encounter). Contamination degrades coordination.

augments.name: Draining Touch
augments.effect: Bleed alternate mode. Toggle freely each hit: instead of dealing bonus damage, each point of Toxicity vented heals you 1 HP. Damage mode or healing mode — choose per swing.

augments.name: Contaminated Blood
augments.effect: When an enemy hits you with a melee attack, they gain +1 Toxicity. Passive. Your blood is toxic. Touch you and it gets in.

augments.name: Toxic Aura
augments.effect: Passive. Enemies starting their turn adjacent to you gain +1 Toxicity. You radiate contamination just by existing. Defensive deterrent and contamination spreader.

content.title: Integrated
content.budget: Three Burns available (Furnace Core +2, Overdrive +3, Reactor State +4). Running all three = +9 Toxicity/round. With Living Furnace reducing each by 1, it's +6/round — but Toxicity never disperses. Corrosive Grasp venting 2 per hit means two hits vent 4. You're still climbing, but slower.

augments.name: Reactor State
augments.effect: New Burn (stacks with Furnace Core and Overdrive). 0 AP: inject +4 Toxicity → gain +1 melee damage die AND damage immunity to the first hit received before your next turn. Three active Burns = +9 Toxicity/round. The damage output is enormous. The climb rate is terrifying.

augments.name: Living Furnace
augments.effect: Trade-off. Toxicity never disperses naturally (not in combat, not between missions — the furnace is always on). In exchange: all Burns cost -1 Toxicity each (minimum 1) AND heal 1 HP per round while above Toxicity 10. Three Burns with Living Furnace = +6/round instead of +9. More sustainable, but you're permanently contaminated.

augments.name: Corrosive Grasp
augments.effect: Bleed upgrade: each melee hit vents 2 Toxicity (instead of 1) as 3 bonus damage (bypasses armor). When a single target reaches 5+ accumulated Toxicity from your Bleeds: -1 Armor for rest of encounter (permanent shred). Your contamination dissolves protection.

augments.name: Contamination Cascade
augments.effect: When you Bleed Toxicity into a target, enemies adjacent to that target each gain 1 Toxicity (splash). Your contamination spreads through proximity. In clustered melee, one hit poisons the group.

augments.name: Siphon Upgrade
augments.effect: Draining Touch healing mode now heals 2 HP per Toxicity vented (instead of 1). At Corrosive Grasp (vent 2/hit), that's 4 HP per melee hit in healing mode. Genuine sustain in prolonged melee.

augments.name: Pressure Release
augments.effect: Once per encounter: on melee hit, vent 1d6 Toxicity instead of normal Bleed. Each point vented deals 2 damage (bypasses armor). At Toxicity 15, a roll of 5 = 10 armor-ignoring damage in one hit plus you dropped 5 Toxicity. The emergency pressure valve.

content.title: Experimental
content.budget: Meltdown is the ultimate Burn — pay one massive injection, then all Burns are free for 3 rounds. During Meltdown, Bleeds are pure profit — every point vented actually reduces your total. The Leech's power spike. The eye of the storm.

augments.name: Meltdown Protocol
augments.effect: New Burn (once per encounter). 0 AP: inject +6 Toxicity. For 3 rounds, all other Burns cost 0 Toxicity. After Meltdown ends: -2 to all checks for 1 round. During Meltdown, you run all Burns every round for free. Bleeds are pure profit. The Leech's power spike. The eye of the storm. Then it ends.

augments.name: Necrotic Field
augments.effect: While above Toxicity 10: Toxic Aura upgrade. Adjacent enemies take 1d4 damage per round in addition to gaining +1 Toxicity. Your contamination is so severe it inflicts physical harm through proximity.

augments.name: Terminal Siphon
augments.effect: On kill (melee only): reduce your Toxicity by 1d4 and heal 1d4 HP. The kill itself vents the reactor. Finishing blows are pressure releases. Mopping up wounded enemies personally is how you manage the post-fight Toxicity curve.

augments.name: Plague Vector
augments.effect: Enemies that die while carrying 5+ Toxicity from your Bleeds explode in a contamination burst. All enemies within 5ft of the corpse gain 3 Toxicity. Your kills spread the plague. In horde fights, chain reactions of contamination cascade through the group.

augments.name: Mutually Assured Destruction
augments.effect: Trade-off. At 0 HP, detonate stored Toxicity. Deal damage equal to your current Toxicity to all creatures within Close range (allies included). Toxicity resets to 0. You're a dead man's switch. Even dying is an attack. The squad knows to stay clear when you're running hot.

augments.name: Patient Zero
augments.effect: Passive. Toxicity you Bleed into enemies is permanent (doesn't fade between encounters — they carry your contamination until treated). Toxicity penalty thresholds shift up by an additional +2 (stacks with Toxic Resilience for +4 total — penalties don't start until Toxicity 10). You cannot collapse from Toxicity (still take penalties and HP drain at 20+, but no unconsciousness or forced Scar). Bleed vents an additional +1 Toxicity per hit (stacks with all Bleed upgrades). You're not a contractor anymore. You're a rift event wearing a person's face.

content.text: QUICK REFERENCE
Burn Stack

rows: +1 damage die, immune to first hit
All other Burns free for 3 rounds

content.text: All Burns active (no Living Furnace): +9 Toxicity/round → +2 damage dice, +2 flat damage, +5ft speed, immune to first hit. All Burns active (with Living Furnace): +6 Toxicity/round → same buffs, but you heal 1 HP/round and Toxicity never disperses.
Bleed Progression

rows: -1 enemy accuracy per 3 Tox on target

content.text: Burn Math Examples

content.label: Full Burns, no Living Furnace, 2 hits/round
content.text: +9 injected - 4 vented (2 hits × 2 Corrosive Grasp) = net +5/round. Over 5 rounds: +25. Unsustainable without Meltdown or kills.

content.label: Full Burns, with Living Furnace, 2 hits/round
content.text: +6 injected - 4 vented = net +2/round. Over 5 rounds: +10. Manageable — you're in the operating range. But Toxicity never disperses.

content.label: During Meltdown (3 rounds), with Living Furnace, 2 hits/round
content.text: Round 1: +5 (Meltdown injection, Living Furnace discount). Burns free for 3 rounds. Rounds 2-3: +0 from Burns. Bleed vents -4/round. Net over Meltdown: +5 - 8 = -3. Meltdown is the ONLY time a Leech decreases Toxicity while running full Burns.

content.label: Miss penalty
content.text: Full Burns + 0 hits = +9 Toxicity, 0 vented. Two bad rounds = +18 with nothing coming back out. Accuracy is survival.
CROSS-BUILDS
Pure Furnace Build (The Reactor)

Furnace Core → Toxic Resilience → Overdrive → Volatile Injection → Reactor State → Living Furnace → Meltdown Protocol → Necrotic Field. Maximum self-buff at all times. Three Burns active = +2 damage dice, +2 flat, +5ft speed, first hit immunity. Living Furnace makes it semi-sustainable. Necrotic Field turns your Toxic Aura into passive 1d4 damage to adjacent enemies. Meltdown Protocol is the spike — 3 rounds of free Burns where every Bleed is pure venting. The problem: you're ALL offense with minimal venting upgrades. Every miss is a crisis. Without Parasite investment, your Bleed is basic — vent 1 per hit for 1 damage. You inject fast and vent slow.
Pure Parasite Build (The Plague)

Furnace Core → Toxin Push → Siphon Strike → Draining Touch → Corrosive Grasp → Contamination Cascade → Terminal Siphon → Plague Vector. Conservative Burns (Furnace Core only, +2/round). Maximum venting — Corrosive Grasp vents 2 per hit for 3 damage each. Draining Touch toggles to healing mode for 4 HP per hit (with Siphon Upgrade). Contamination Cascade splashes Toxicity to adjacent enemies. Terminal Siphon vents 1d4 on every kill. Plague Vector makes kills explode in contamination bursts. The problem: modest Burns mean modest buffs. Your damage per hit is lower than a Furnace build. You compensate with sustain and consistency.
Hybrid Build (The Full Leech)

Furnace Core → Toxin Push → Overdrive → Siphon Strike → Corrosive Grasp → Living Furnace → Contamination Cascade → Meltdown Protocol → Terminal Siphon. Both branches at depth. Burns inject +6/round (Living Furnace discount). Corrosive Grasp vents 2/hit × 2 hits = -4/round. Net climb: +2/round. Contamination Cascade splashes to adjacent enemies. Terminal Siphon vents on kills. Meltdown Protocol gives 3 rounds of free Burns for pure venting profit. Over a 6-round fight: climb ~12 Toxicity total (manageable with Toxic Resilience shifting thresholds). You're the complete Leech — strong buffs, efficient venting, contamination spreading, kill-based recovery.
Leech + Sin Eater Cross-Tree

Leech T3 (Furnace Core, Overdrive, Reactor State) + Sin Eater T2 (Empathic Link, Transfer Efficiency). The overflow valve synergy. Leech runs all three Burns (+9/round). Sin Eater ally uses Deep Cleanse to pull Toxicity off the Leech between combats (or during with Mass Absorb). The Leech gets to run hotter because someone else is managing the excess. The Sin Eater gains fuel for Redirect from the Leech's Toxicity. Symbiotic — the Leech is the engine, the Sin Eater is the exhaust system.
Leech + Shadow Cross-Tree

Shadow T2 (Sprint Actuators, charge bonuses) + Leech T2 (Furnace Core, Toxin Push). The contamination blitz. Shadow Pinball trigger (charge → bonus damage → 5 free feet → charge next target). Every hit applies Toxic Touch (+1 Tox) + Bleed (vent 1 Tox, 1 damage). You're charging through enemy lines leaving a trail of contamination. Overdrive's +5ft speed helps enable longer charges. The problem: Shadow wants Reflex/Edge, Leech wants Grit/Chrome. The stat spread is painful. You do both at half strength.
Leech + Fury Cross-Tree

Fury T2 (Adrenal Injector, rage buffs) + Leech T2 (Furnace Core, Overdrive). Double melee damage stacking. Fury gives rage-based damage bonuses. Leech gives Burn-based damage bonuses. Both want Chrome and both want to be in melee. The damage output is the highest melee damage in the game — but you have no accuracy bonuses from either tree, your Toxicity climbs from Leech Burns, and Fury's recklessness compounds with Leech's Toxicity penalties. Glass cannon melee. Hit hard, die spectacularly.
Leech + Fortress Cross-Tree

Fortress T2 (Subdermal Plating, Reactive Plating) + Leech T2 (Furnace Core, Toxic Aura). The contamination tank. Fortress armor lets you survive the melee you need for Bleed venting. Reactive Plating + Contaminated Blood means enemies that hit you take thorns damage AND gain Toxicity. Toxic Aura poisons adjacent enemies every round. You're a durable contamination zone — hard to kill, constantly poisoning everything near you. The problem: Fortress wants to be defensive. Leech wants to be aggressive. You're tanky but your damage is moderate because neither tree is at full depth.
Leech + Precision Cross-Tree

Precision T2 (Targeting Suite, accuracy bonuses) + Leech T2 (Furnace Core, Toxin Push). The guided warhead. Leech's critical weakness is no accuracy. Precision fixes it. Targeting Suite, Marksman bonuses, and Cracked armor shred give the Leech what they desperately need: the guarantee that Burn injections get vented through hits. The most mathematically efficient Leech build — and the least thematic. The problem: Precision wants you stationary and ranged. Leech wants you mobile and melee. You'd need to alternate: ranged Marksman turns for accuracy, melee Leech turns for Bleed. Functional but schizophrenic.
DESIGN NOTES

Why Burn + Bleed instead of a generic Toxicity system: The two-part loop creates genuine tension. Burns are deliberate — you CHOOSE to inject. Bleeds are conditional — you can only vent on HIT. This asymmetry is the entire class. If Burns and Bleeds were both voluntary, there'd be no tension. If both were conditional, there'd be no agency. The Leech has complete control over intake and zero control over output (except hitting things). That's the plate-spinning.

Why no accuracy from the tree: This is the most controversial design decision and the most important one. Every other melee class can self-improve accuracy: Fury has rage synergies, Shadow has charge bonuses, Dancer has volume-of-fire escalation. The Leech gets NONE of that from their own tree. A Leech who misses is a reactor with no cooling system. This creates genuine party dependency and cross-tree investment pressure. Without this constraint, the Leech would be a self-sufficient damage monster with no weaknesses.

Why Living Furnace is the defining trade-off: Living Furnace (-1 Toxicity per Burn, heal 1 HP/round above 10, but Toxicity NEVER disperses) is the single most impactful augment decision in the game. Without it: Burns are expensive but Toxicity disperses naturally between combats. With it: Burns are cheaper and you self-heal, but you walk into every mission carrying whatever Toxicity you ended last mission with. Living Furnace Leeches have a permanent relationship with contamination — they're never clean. The narrative and mechanical implications ripple across the entire campaign.

Why Patient Zero makes Toxicity permanent: At T5, the Leech has invested 10 augments into being a contamination engine. The mastery reward is that their contamination is PERMANENT on enemies — it doesn't fade between encounters. An enemy the Leech poisoned in mission 4 is still poisoned in mission 5 (if they survive). This is narratively enormous — the Leech is a plague vector, not just a fighter. Mechanically, it means enemies the Leech has fought before start encounters already contaminated.

Why Meltdown is the power spike: Meltdown (+6 Toxicity, all other Burns free for 3 rounds) creates the only window where a Leech can actually DECREASE Toxicity while running full Burns. During Meltdown: +0 from Burns (free), -4 from Bleeds (assuming 2 hits). Net: -4/round. The Leech is cooling down while at maximum power. This is the eye of the storm — 3 rounds of peak performance with no climb. Then it ends and you're at +6 from the initial injection plus wherever you were before.

Why Mutually Assured Destruction exists: The Leech at 0 HP is sitting on a pile of Toxicity with nothing to do with it. MAD converts that Toxicity into damage — but to EVERYONE in range, including allies. This creates a genuine tactical consideration: do you invest in the dead man's switch knowing your squad needs to stay clear when you go down? The Leech is already a melee-range hazard (Toxic Aura, Contaminated Blood). MAD makes them dangerous even in death.

content.label: AP Math — Typical Leech Turns (5 AP)
items: Standard Burn & Bleed: Burn (0 AP, +2 Tox, +1 damage die) → Move to melee range (1 AP) → Attack (2 AP, Bleed on hit) → Attack (2 AP, Bleed on hit). Net: +2 Toxicity injected, -2 vented (if both hit), 2 attacks with +1 damage die.

Full Burns (T3): Furnace Core + Overdrive + Reactor State (all 0 AP, +9 Tox) → Attack (2 AP) → Attack (2 AP) → Move or Shield (1 AP). Net: +9 injected, -4 vented (Corrosive Grasp, 2 hits). Enormous damage. Climbing +5/round.

Meltdown Turn: Meltdown (0 AP, +6 Tox, activates free Burns) → Attack (2 AP) → Attack (2 AP) → Contaminating Blow (1 AP, next attack +3 Tox to target). Net: +6 from Meltdown, Burns free, Bleeds venting. Maximum output, beginning of the eye of the storm.

Desperation (missed last round, high Tox): Vent (1 AP, -2 Tox) → Attack (2 AP, please hit) → Attack (2 AP, please hit). No Burns this round. Pure venting attempt. If both hit at Corrosive Grasp: -6 Toxicity total. If both miss: -2 from Vent only. Pray for accuracy.

Kill Chain: Attack (2 AP, kill → Terminal Siphon -1d4 Tox, heal 1d4) → Attack (2 AP, kill → Terminal Siphon again) → Move (1 AP). Burns still active from last round. Two kills = potentially -8 Toxicity from Terminal Siphon + Bleeds. The best turns involve killing things — kills are the Leech's primary Toxicity management.
